name: Architecture View
description:
VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel ("Architecture") that renders the architecture wiki as a searchable list of component cards. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui ArchPanel React component and answers its RPC calls (list/upsert/remove/openCard/search) against the repo's .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json cards — the SAME store the bundled arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) reads and writes, so cards Claude authors via seed-arch/arch_upsert and cards edited in the panel are one and the same files. The RPC handler set is built by buildArchRpcHandlers(ctx, getRepoRoot) exported from archView.ts and shared with the full-page board (archPage.ts). The .arch FileSystemWatcher is owned by ArchViewProvider itself (created in the constructor and re-pointed in onRepoChanged) — NOT by the webview lifecycle — so external card writes refresh both the sidebar and the full page even in a window where the sidebar view was never resolved; onCardsChanged (wired to refreshArchPage) fans the watcher out to the page. The page does not re-push 'arch-changed' on reveal/visible — the watcher is the only change signal, which keeps the panel from re-running semantic search for identical results. Originally ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane; the force-directed ArchGraph was removed.

ArchPanel has two surfaces distinguished by a pageMode prop (like TasksPanel): the sidebar is narrow and clicking a card opens its <slug>.json file in the normal editor (openCard RPC → showTextDocument). The full-page board (archPage posts context {surface:'page'} → arch.tsx sets pageMode) renders a master/detail layout: a capped list column plus an in-webview read-only ArchDetail viewer (name, slug, tags/dependsOn chips, description, Files (mono), Guidelines, Anti-patterns, Decisions sections, created/updated meta). In page mode selecting a card shows the detail pane instead of opening the file; an "Open .json" button still opens the raw file to edit, and a Delete button removes the card. dependsOn renders as chips that navigate to the linked card (disabled for unknown slugs).
guidelines: Both sidebar and full-page board share one ArchPanel bundle; branch behavior on the pageMode prop derived from context {surface:'page'} rather than forking the component.

In page mode do NOT call api.openCard on select (that opens the raw JSON); show the ArchDetail pane. Keep openCard for the explicit 'Open .json' button and for card creation (which needs the file to edit).
Detail viewer is read-only — editing stays in the .json file. Use decodeEntities on all card text before display (names/descriptions may contain HTML entities).

New CSS classes are prefixed arch-* and reuse the shared theme tokens (--bg-card/--bg-1/--rule/--border/--fg-*/--clay); the webviewTheme contract test requires every var(--…) in styles.css be declared.
anti_patterns: Don't add a full in-webview edit form — the card file in the normal editor is the single edit surface (the old Electron CardEditor was deliberately removed).
Don't push 'arch-changed' on page reveal/visible — it re-runs semantic search for identical results; rely on the ArchViewProvider file watcher.
decisions: The Architecture panel was ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane into the shared ui package so it renders identically; the Electron useArchWiki/window.api hook was replaced by an injected ArchApi adapter wired over the webview postMessage RPC bridge (webviewRpc.ts / bridge.ts).
The force-directed ArchGraph nodes view was removed; the panel is now a search + list only.

The in-webview CardEditor form was removed (commit f04702d, feat: add openCard). Cards are now edited by opening their <slug>.json file in the normal editor via the openCard RPC; the old openFile RPC and the read-only detail pane were dropped with it.
Live refresh uses a VS Code FileSystemWatcher instead of the Electron app's chokidar + coalesce watcher; event debouncing is delegated to VS Code's watcher.
The watcher moved from resolveWebviewView into the ArchViewProvider constructor so the full-page board refreshes without the sidebar ever being resolved (review fix on afba156).
